Klavis-AI/klavis ·GitHub ⭐ 5661 Python · AI Agents, MCP, Context Optimization, Developer Tools, SaaS, SDK, Open Source

What it is

Klavis provides a platform for managing and optimizing AI agent interactions with external tools. Its core component, Strata, serves as an intelligent connector that handles thousands of tools while optimizing the LLM context window through efficient tool selection and aggregation.

Problems solved

  • Context window bloat from large tool definitions
  • Complexity of managing hundreds of MCP integrations
  • Lack of scalable, isolated sandboxes for LLM training and RL environments

How to get started

Access cloud-hosted services at klavis.ai or self-host via Docker. Use the Python/TypeScript SDKs to create 'Strata' instances that bundle multiple toolsets (Slack, Gmail, etc.) into a single, optimized interface for your AI agent.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

Klavis-AI/klavis is positioned as a klavis provides a platform for managing and optimizing ai agent interactions with external tools. its core component, strata, serves as an intelligent connector that handles thousands of tools while optimizing the llm context window through efficient tool selection and aggregation. It aims to provide developers with a robust, Python-based solution for klavis ai (yc x25): mcp integration platforms that let ai agents use tools reliably at any scale.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Context window bloat from large tool definitions. Klavis-AI/klavis addresses this by klavis provides a platform for managing and optimizing ai agent interactions with external tools. its core component, strata, serves as an intelligent connector that handles thousands of tools while optimizing the llm context window through efficient tool selection and aggregation, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using Python, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around klavis ai (yc x25): mcp integration platforms that let ai agents use tools reliably at any scale. The implementation utilizes Python's strengths to provide klavis provides a platform for managing and optimizing ai agent interactions with external tools. its core component, strata, serves as an intelligent connector that handles thousands of tools while optimizing the llm context window through efficient tool selection and aggregation, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, access cloud-hosted services at klavis.ai or self-host via docker. use the python/typescript sdks to create 'strata' instances that bundle multiple toolsets (slack, gmail, etc.) into a single, optimized interface for your ai agent. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, Python-powered utility that streamlines klavis ai (yc x25): mcp integration platforms that let ai agents use tools reliably at any scale; it effectively tackles Context window bloat from large tool definitions while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
